calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment Balance With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LYLE D. SOLOMON, Esq. of Law Office of Lyle D. Solomon (Tel: 916-532-2726) for plaintiff Kelstin Group, Inc., dba Pacific Credit Services;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KELSTIN GROUP, INC. DBA PACIFIC CREDIT SERVICES' Motion To Amend Judgment Balance.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's mistitled "motion to amend judgment balance" is denied. By judgment entered October 18, 2019, the Court granted a default judgment in favor of plaintiff in the amount of $4,609.13. Plaintiff now seeks to set aside that judgment under Section 473(b) of the Code of Civil Procedure, contending that it inadvertently failed to appear at the prove up hearing set for January 9, 2020, but did appear the next day and found out that the case had been dismissed due to its failure to appear. However, no such scheduled hearing or dismissal appears on the court's docket. Plaintiff admits that "[t]he amount on the judgment was listed incorrectly when submitted to the courts." (Solomon Decl. par. 3.) It now belatedly seeks to amend the judgment to reflect the "correct" judgment balance, which it apparently contends (without any supporting evidence) should be $7,419.13, the amount it originally sought in October 2019. Plaintiff's motion is untimely, having been filed more than one year after the expiration of the 6-month period specified in section 473(b), which is jurisdictional. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 473(b) [application "shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken"]; Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["The six-month time limit for granting statutory relief is jurisdictional and the court may not consider a motion for relief made after that period has elapsed."].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Off Calendar
